Associated Deities: {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta no Mikoto|ja|太田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} |SacredObject = |Rank = {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
[[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]
{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}
{{ill|Imperial offerings shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial offerings shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Buretsu |MainHall = {{ill|Miya-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Alias = |Festival = June 10 |LabelPosition = left |Map = Japan Fukui |location = {{ill|Ikeda|qid=Q1347548}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.88622222222222|N|136.3455277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Suwaazuki Shrine''' (須波阿湏疑神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Ikeda Town, Fukui|Ikeda Town]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]], Japan. The character "湏" (water radical + 頁) is not included in [[:en:JIS X 0208|JIS X 0208]], so the alternative representation "'''須波阿須疑神社'''" is sometimes used online, though it represents a different character. == Overview == Suwaazuki Shrine is identified as a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Suwaazuki Shrine Sanza (Echizen Province, Imadate District)" listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. It is classified as a small shrine in the Shikinaisha system and designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Cultural Properties == ; Main Hall : Built in the {{ill|Miya-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} style, it measures 4.88 meters in length (桁行) and 3.15 meters in width (梁間). It features a {{ill|Hiwadabuki|ja|檜皮葺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiwadabuki|qq=|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}} roof with a three-bay {{ill|Mokoshi|ja|向拝|1=kōhai|2=ja|3=向拝|4=zh|5=向拜|lt=kōhai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/kōhai (architecture)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415970}}. Constructed during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], it is the oldest shrine main hall in Fukui Prefecture. Designated as a {{ill|National Treasure of Japan (pre-1950)|ja|旧国宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Treasure of Japan (pre-1950)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国宝|1=National Treasure of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1139795}} under the 1941 National Treasure Preservation Law, it is now an Important Cultural Property under the 1950 Cultural Property Protection Law.Ministry of Education Notification No. 823, November 6, 1941 ; Worship Hall : Built during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] in 1689 (Genroku 2), it has a gabled roof, originally thatched and now copper-tiled. Measuring 8 by 5.5 bays, it has plain wooden paneling. The hall functioned as a [[:en:Noh|Noh]] stage until 1917 (Taishō 5) and is designated as a cultural property by the town. ; Gate (Rōmon) : A three-bay, one-entrance {{ill|Rōmon|ja|楼門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōmon|qq=|1=Rōmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378762}} with a gabled roof and copper tiles, originally thatched. Built in the late Edo period, it features white plastered walls and vermillion-painted wood, reflecting [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] practices. It is also designated a cultural property by the town. == Features == ; Shrine Treasures : A Noh mask of "Tenjin," dedicated in 1571 (Genki 2) by Chiyokumamaru from Nojiri, is recognized as an Important Art Object.Ministry of Education Notification No. 148, May 28, 1949 ; Noh Mask Festival : Although Noh performances are no longer held, an annual "Noh Mask Festival" takes place on February 6, featuring offerings of azuki porridge to visitors. ; Inari Giant Cedar : The sacred tree "Inari Giant Cedar" stands on the hillside behind the main hall. Dedicated to {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, it is named "Aratama Giant Cedar Daimyōjin." Estimated at 500 years old, it stands 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of 8.6 meters and a root spread of 11.7 meters. It is a Fukui Prefecture Natural Monument. == Religious Events == ; festival (Ikeda Grand Festival) : Held over three days around June 10, this festival has been celebrated since before the Edo period to pray for abundant harvests. * June 9: Performance of "Ikeda Oiwake," a Fukui Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * June 10 (main day): Morning prayer offering and tamagushi ritual; afternoon performances, including "Urayasu no Mai," sumo wrestling, and taiko drumming by groups from Echizen City. == History == The shrine was traditionally founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] with a division of {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}} from [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. Later, it enshrined the local deity "Azuki Kami" (Ōnotehime) and added Ukanomitama (Inari deity) in 716 (Reiki 2, Nara period). The main hall houses Ukanomitama, with Takeminakata and Ōnotehime in the right and left halls, respectively, and associated deities Ōta no Mikoto and Ōnamuchi. Historically, the shrine served as the communal shrine (Sōsha) of 48 villages in Ikeda and was revered by successive rulers. During the late Muromachi period, war caused a decline until 1491 (Entoku 3), when the shrine was restored by Ikeda Tokitada, a retainer of {{ill|Asakura Sadakage|ja|朝倉貞景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Sadakage|qq=|1=Asakura Sadakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517337}}. The current main hall dates from this restoration. However, in 1574 (Tenshō 2), the [[:en:Ikkō-ikki|Ikkō-ikki]] uprising destroyed most buildings and treasures, leaving only the main hall. Subsequent restoration began in 1595 (Bunroku 4), with the worship hall rebuilt by 1600 (Keichō 5). Silver donations from lords supported further repairs during the Edo period. The current gate was completed in 1850 (Kaei 3). The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1873 (Meiji 6) and became an Imperial offerings shrine in 1908 (Meiji 41). == location == Address: Fukui Prefecture, Imadate District, Ikeda Town, Inari 13-1 == Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Takefu Station|Takefu Station]], [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]]. ** Bus: Take the Ikeda Town route, disembark at "Inari," 2 minutes on foot. * By car: About 30 minutes from [[Takefu Interchange]] or [[Sabae Interchange]] on the [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]], or 35 minutes from [[Fukui Interchange]]. == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Noh Museum|ja|能楽の里歴史館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noh Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Displays related to Noh culture. * {{ill|Ukamaji Shrine|ja|鵜甘神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukamaji Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Known for its traditional dance. * {{ill|Horiguchi House|ja|堀口家住宅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horiguchi House|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Edo period structure. * {{ill|Kazura Bridge|ja|かずら橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazura Bridge|qq=|1=Kazura bridge at the Iya Valley|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11263836}} - Scenic vine suspension bridge. * {{ill|Ikeda Folklore Museum|ja|池田町民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Folklore Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Umada Clan Garden|ja|梅田氏庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umada Clan Garden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Historic garden of the Umada family. * {{ill|Noh Mask Art Museum|ja|能面美術館（福井県）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noh Mask Art Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.genbu.net/data/etizen/suhaasugi_title.htm Suwaazuki Shrine] - Genbushi no Kioku * [http://www.e-ikeda.jp/see/p004020.html Suwaazuki Shrine & Inari Giant Cedar] - Ikeda Town Tourism Information {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Fukui Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|須波阿湏疑神社|version=102326784|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Subasukiji Shrine]][[sn:Subasukiji Shrine]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] }} [[sn:Subasukiji Shrine]] [[ja:須波阿湏疑神社]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suwaazuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suwaazuki shrine gate covered snow.jpg|220px]]
Torii Gate | location = Inari 13-1, Ikeda Town, Imadate District, Fukui Prefecture, Japan | ISO = JP-18 | Latitude = 35.8862 | Longitude = 136.3455 | deity = Right Hall: {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}
Main Hall: {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}
Left Hall: {{ill|Ōnotehime-no-Kami|ja|大野手比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnotehime-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Accompanying Deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Okuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|qq=}} | Classification = {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kensha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|Shrines receiving offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines receiving offerings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Buretsu | Architecture = Three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Festival = [[:en:June 10|June 10]] | Map = Japan Fukui | Label = left }} '''Suwaazuki Shrine''' (須波阿湏疑神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ikeda Town (Fukui)|ja|池田町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Town (Fukui)|qq=|1=Ikeda Town (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206815}}, Imadate District, Fukui Prefecture. The character "湏" (water radical + 頁) is not included in the [[:en:JIS X 0208|JIS X 0208]] character set, and the shrine is often referred to as "須波阿須疑神社" as a substitute notation online. == Overview == The shrine is considered the location of the "Suwaazuki Shrine Three Halls" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a small-scale {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. It was classified as a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kensha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Cultural Properties == === Main Hall === The Main Hall is a three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure, measuring 4.88 meters across the beams and 3.15 meters along the depth. It features a cypress bark roof and a three-bay porch. Built during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], it is the oldest shrine building in Fukui Prefecture and was designated as a National Treasure under the pre-war {{ill|National Treasure Preservation Law|ja|国宝保存法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Treasure Preservation Law|qq=|1=National Treasures Preservation Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420716}} in 1941. It has since been reclassified as an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Act|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Act|qq=|1=Act on Protection of Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188502}} in 1950.Showa 16 (1941), Ministry of Education Notification No. 823 === Worship Hall === The current Worship Hall was built in [[:en:1689|1689]] during the early Edo period. It features an irimoya (hip-and-gable) roof covered with copper sheeting (formerly thatched) and has a unique open layout used for Noh performances. It is designated as a cultural property by the town. === Gate (Rōmon) === The gate is a three-bay, single-entry structure with a hipped roof. The design reflects a combination of Shinto and Buddhist architectural styles. Built in the late Edo period, it is also recognized as a cultural property by the town. == Special Features == === Shrine Treasures === The shrine possesses a Noh mask called "Tenjin," which was donated in [[:en:1571|1571]]. This mask is classified as an Important Cultural Property.Showa 24 (1949), Ministry of Education Notification No. 148 === Noh Mask Festival === Although Noh performances are no longer held, the shrine organizes an annual "Noh Mask Festival" on [[:en:February 6|February 6]], where visitors are served azuki bean porridge. === Great Cedar of Inari === The sacred tree "Great Cedar of Inari" is located on the hillside behind the Main Hall. Estimated to be 500 years old, it is one of the most notable trees in the Hokuriku region and is designated a Natural Monument by Fukui Prefecture. == Festivals == === Grand Festival (Ikeda Grand Festival) === The Grand Festival is held annually around [[:en:June 10|June 10]] over three days. It includes traditional events like the offering of prayers for bountiful harvests. * June 9: Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property "Ikeda Oiwake." * June 10 (main day): Shinto rituals, including norito recitation and tamagushi offerings. * June 10–11: Various performances, including the "Dance of Peace" and taiko drum performances. == History == The shrine was traditionally founded during the reign of Emperor Buretsu by enshrining a divided spirit of {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} from the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. In the Nara period, local deity {{ill|Onotehime|ja|大野手比売|1=Ōnotehime-no-Kami|2=ja|3=大野手比売|lt=Ōnotehime-no-Kami|lt=Ōnotehime-no-Kami|ja|lt2=大野手比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onotehime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnotehime-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (an [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari deity]]) were also enshrined. Despite setbacks during the Sengoku period, including destruction by the [[:en:Ikkō-ikki|Ikkō-ikki]], the shrine was later reconstructed. In 1873, it was designated a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kensha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and became a shrine receiving state offerings in 1908. == location == '''Address:''' * Inari 13-1, Ikeda Town, Imadate District, Fukui Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation:''' * By train: Take the JR West Hokuriku Main Line to [[:en:Takefu Station|Takefu Station]]; from there, a bus to "Inari" and a two-minute walk. * By car: Accessible from the [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]] via Takefu or Sabae Interchanges (30 minutes). == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Nogaku no Sato Historical Museum|ja|能楽の里歴史館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogaku no Sato Historical Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Displays traditional Noh masks and costumes. * {{ill|Ugami Shrine|ja|鵜甘神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Features a unique tradition of Dengaku dance. * {{ill|Horiguchi Family Residence|ja|堀口家住宅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horiguchi Family Residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Edo-period architecture linked to the shrine. * {{ill|Kazura Bridge|ja|かずら橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazura Bridge|qq=|1=Kazura bridge at the Iya Valley|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11263836}} – A scenic suspension bridge. * {{ill|Ikeda Town Folklore Museum|ja|池田町民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Town Folklore Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Exhibits traditional agricultural tools and daily life artifacts. * {{ill|Umeda Family Garden|ja|梅田氏庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeda Family Garden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Historic garden of the Umeda family. == References == Associated Deities: {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta no Mikoto|ja|太田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} |SacredObject = |Rank = {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
[[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]
{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}
{{ill|Imperial offerings shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial offerings shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Buretsu |MainHall = {{ill|Miya-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Alias = |Festival = June 10 |LabelPosition = left |Map = Japan Fukui |location = {{ill|Ikeda|qid=Q1347548}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.88622222222222|N|136.3455277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Suwaazuki Shrine''' (須波阿湏疑神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Ikeda Town, Fukui|Ikeda Town]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]], Japan. The character "湏" (water radical + 頁) is not included in [[:en:JIS X 0208|JIS X 0208]], so the alternative representation "'''須波阿須疑神社'''" is sometimes used online, though it represents a different character. == Overview == Suwaazuki Shrine is identified as a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Suwaazuki Shrine Sanza (Echizen Province, Imadate District)" listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. It is classified as a small shrine in the Shikinaisha system and designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Cultural Properties == ; Main Hall : Built in the {{ill|Miya-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} style, it measures 4.88 meters in length (桁行) and 3.15 meters in width (梁間). It features a {{ill|Hiwadabuki|ja|檜皮葺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiwadabuki|qq=|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}} roof with a three-bay {{ill|Mokoshi|ja|向拝|1=kōhai|2=ja|3=向拝|4=zh|5=向拜|lt=kōhai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/kōhai (architecture)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415970}}. Constructed during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], it is the oldest shrine main hall in Fukui Prefecture. Designated as a {{ill|National Treasure of Japan (pre-1950)|ja|旧国宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Treasure of Japan (pre-1950)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国宝|1=National Treasure of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1139795}} under the 1941 National Treasure Preservation Law, it is now an Important Cultural Property under the 1950 Cultural Property Protection Law.Ministry of Education Notification No. 823, November 6, 1941 ; Worship Hall : Built during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] in 1689 (Genroku 2), it has a gabled roof, originally thatched and now copper-tiled. Measuring 8 by 5.5 bays, it has plain wooden paneling. The hall functioned as a [[:en:Noh|Noh]] stage until 1917 (Taishō 5) and is designated as a cultural property by the town. ; Gate (Rōmon) : A three-bay, one-entrance {{ill|Rōmon|ja|楼門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōmon|qq=|1=Rōmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378762}} with a gabled roof and copper tiles, originally thatched. Built in the late Edo period, it features white plastered walls and vermillion-painted wood, reflecting [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] practices. It is also designated a cultural property by the town. == Features == ; Shrine Treasures : A Noh mask of "Tenjin," dedicated in 1571 (Genki 2) by Chiyokumamaru from Nojiri, is recognized as an Important Art Object.Ministry of Education Notification No. 148, May 28, 1949 ; Noh Mask Festival : Although Noh performances are no longer held, an annual "Noh Mask Festival" takes place on February 6, featuring offerings of azuki porridge to visitors. ; Inari Giant Cedar : The sacred tree "Inari Giant Cedar" stands on the hillside behind the main hall. Dedicated to {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, it is named "Aratama Giant Cedar Daimyōjin." Estimated at 500 years old, it stands 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of 8.6 meters and a root spread of 11.7 meters. It is a Fukui Prefecture Natural Monument. == Religious Events == ; festival (Ikeda Grand Festival) : Held over three days around June 10, this festival has been celebrated since before the Edo period to pray for abundant harvests. * June 9: Performance of "Ikeda Oiwake," a Fukui Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * June 10 (main day): Morning prayer offering and tamagushi ritual; afternoon performances, including "Urayasu no Mai," sumo wrestling, and taiko drumming by groups from Echizen City. == History == The shrine was traditionally founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] with a division of {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}} from [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. Later, it enshrined the local deity "Azuki Kami" (Ōnotehime) and added Ukanomitama (Inari deity) in 716 (Reiki 2, Nara period). The main hall houses Ukanomitama, with Takeminakata and Ōnotehime in the right and left halls, respectively, and associated deities Ōta no Mikoto and Ōnamuchi. Historically, the shrine served as the communal shrine (Sōsha) of 48 villages in Ikeda and was revered by successive rulers. During the late Muromachi period, war caused a decline until 1491 (Entoku 3), when the shrine was restored by Ikeda Tokitada, a retainer of {{ill|Asakura Sadakage|ja|朝倉貞景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Sadakage|qq=|1=Asakura Sadakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517337}}. The current main hall dates from this restoration. However, in 1574 (Tenshō 2), the [[:en:Ikkō-ikki|Ikkō-ikki]] uprising destroyed most buildings and treasures, leaving only the main hall. Subsequent restoration began in 1595 (Bunroku 4), with the worship hall rebuilt by 1600 (Keichō 5). Silver donations from lords supported further repairs during the Edo period. The current gate was completed in 1850 (Kaei 3). The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1873 (Meiji 6) and became an Imperial offerings shrine in 1908 (Meiji 41). == location == Address: Fukui Prefecture, Imadate District, Ikeda Town, Inari 13-1 == Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Takefu Station|Takefu Station]], [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]]. ** Bus: Take the Ikeda Town route, disembark at "Inari," 2 minutes on foot. * By car: About 30 minutes from [[Takefu Interchange]] or [[Sabae Interchange]] on the [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]], or 35 minutes from [[Fukui Interchange]]. == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Noh Museum|ja|能楽の里歴史館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noh Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Displays related to Noh culture. * {{ill|Ukamaji Shrine|ja|鵜甘神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukamaji Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Known for its traditional dance. * {{ill|Horiguchi House|ja|堀口家住宅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horiguchi House|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Edo period structure. * {{ill|Kazura Bridge|ja|かずら橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazura Bridge|qq=|1=Kazura bridge at the Iya Valley|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11263836}} - Scenic vine suspension bridge. * {{ill|Ikeda Folklore Museum|ja|池田町民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Folklore Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Umada Clan Garden|ja|梅田氏庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umada Clan Garden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Historic garden of the Umada family. * {{ill|Noh Mask Art Museum|ja|能面美術館（福井県）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noh Mask Art Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.genbu.net/data/etizen/suhaasugi_title.htm Suwaazuki Shrine] - Genbushi no Kioku * [http://www.e-ikeda.jp/see/p004020.html Suwaazuki Shrine & Inari Giant Cedar] - Ikeda Town Tourism Information {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Fukui Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|須波阿湏疑神社|version=102326784|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Subasukiji Shrine]][[sn:Subasukiji Shrine]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] }} [[sn:Subasukiji Shrine]] [[ja:須波阿湏疑神社]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suwaazuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suwaazuki shrine gate covered snow.jpg|220px]]
Torii Gate | location = Inari 13-1, Ikeda Town, Imadate District, Fukui Prefecture, Japan | ISO = JP-18 | Latitude = 35.8862 | Longitude = 136.3455 | deity = Right Hall: {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}
Main Hall: {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}
Left Hall: {{ill|Ōnotehime-no-Kami|ja|大野手比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnotehime-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Accompanying Deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Okuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|qq=}} | Classification = {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kensha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|Shrines receiving offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines receiving offerings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Buretsu | Architecture = Three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Festival = [[:en:June 10|June 10]] | Map = Japan Fukui | Label = left }} '''Suwaazuki Shrine''' (須波阿湏疑神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ikeda Town (Fukui)|ja|池田町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Town (Fukui)|qq=|1=Ikeda Town (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206815}}, Imadate District, Fukui Prefecture. The character "湏" (water radical + 頁) is not included in the [[:en:JIS X 0208|JIS X 0208]] character set, and the shrine is often referred to as "須波阿須疑神社" as a substitute notation online. == Overview == The shrine is considered the location of the "Suwaazuki Shrine Three Halls" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a small-scale {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. It was classified as a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kensha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Cultural Properties == === Main Hall === The Main Hall is a three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure, measuring 4.88 meters across the beams and 3.15 meters along the depth. It features a cypress bark roof and a three-bay porch. Built during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], it is the oldest shrine building in Fukui Prefecture and was designated as a National Treasure under the pre-war {{ill|National Treasure Preservation Law|ja|国宝保存法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Treasure Preservation Law|qq=|1=National Treasures Preservation Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420716}} in 1941. It has since been reclassified as an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Act|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Act|qq=|1=Act on Protection of Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188502}} in 1950.Showa 16 (1941), Ministry of Education Notification No. 823 === Worship Hall === The current Worship Hall was built in [[:en:1689|1689]] during the early Edo period. It features an irimoya (hip-and-gable) roof covered with copper sheeting (formerly thatched) and has a unique open layout used for Noh performances. It is designated as a cultural property by the town. === Gate (Rōmon) === The gate is a three-bay, single-entry structure with a hipped roof. The design reflects a combination of Shinto and Buddhist architectural styles. Built in the late Edo period, it is also recognized as a cultural property by the town. == Special Features == === Shrine Treasures === The shrine possesses a Noh mask called "Tenjin," which was donated in [[:en:1571|1571]]. This mask is classified as an Important Cultural Property.Showa 24 (1949), Ministry of Education Notification No. 148 === Noh Mask Festival === Although Noh performances are no longer held, the shrine organizes an annual "Noh Mask Festival" on [[:en:February 6|February 6]], where visitors are served azuki bean porridge. === Great Cedar of Inari === The sacred tree "Great Cedar of Inari" is located on the hillside behind the Main Hall. Estimated to be 500 years old, it is one of the most notable trees in the Hokuriku region and is designated a Natural Monument by Fukui Prefecture. == Festivals == === Grand Festival (Ikeda Grand Festival) === The Grand Festival is held annually around [[:en:June 10|June 10]] over three days. It includes traditional events like the offering of prayers for bountiful harvests. * June 9: Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property "Ikeda Oiwake." * June 10 (main day): Shinto rituals, including norito recitation and tamagushi offerings. * June 10–11: Various performances, including the "Dance of Peace" and taiko drum performances. == History == The shrine was traditionally founded during the reign of Emperor Buretsu by enshrining a divided spirit of {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} from the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. In the Nara period, local deity {{ill|Onotehime|ja|大野手比売|1=Ōnotehime-no-Kami|2=ja|3=大野手比売|lt=Ōnotehime-no-Kami|lt=Ōnotehime-no-Kami|ja|lt2=大野手比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onotehime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnotehime-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (an [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari deity]]) were also enshrined. Despite setbacks during the Sengoku period, including destruction by the [[:en:Ikkō-ikki|Ikkō-ikki]], the shrine was later reconstructed. In 1873, it was designated a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kensha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and became a shrine receiving state offerings in 1908. == location == '''Address:''' * Inari 13-1, Ikeda Town, Imadate District, Fukui Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation:''' * By train: Take the JR West Hokuriku Main Line to [[:en:Takefu Station|Takefu Station]]; from there, a bus to "Inari" and a two-minute walk. * By car: Accessible from the [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]] via Takefu or Sabae Interchanges (30 minutes). == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Nogaku no Sato Historical Museum|ja|能楽の里歴史館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogaku no Sato Historical Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Displays traditional Noh masks and costumes. * {{ill|Ugami Shrine|ja|鵜甘神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Features a unique tradition of Dengaku dance. * {{ill|Horiguchi Family Residence|ja|堀口家住宅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horiguchi Family Residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Edo-period architecture linked to the shrine. * {{ill|Kazura Bridge|ja|かずら橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazura Bridge|qq=|1=Kazura bridge at the Iya Valley|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11263836}} – A scenic suspension bridge. * {{ill|Ikeda Town Folklore Museum|ja|池田町民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Town Folklore Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Exhibits traditional agricultural tools and daily life artifacts. * {{ill|Umeda Family Garden|ja|梅田氏庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeda Family Garden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Historic garden of the Umeda family. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.genbu.net/data/etizen/suhaasugi_title.htm Suwaazuki Shrine – Genbushiko's Memories] * [http://www.e-ikeda.jp/see/p004020.html Suwaazuki Shrine & Inari Cedar – Ikeda Tourism Information] {{translated page|ja|須波阿湏疑神社|version=102326784|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] }} [[sn:Sunahaasu Shrine]] [[ja:須波阿湏疑神社]] [[sn:Subasukiji Shrine]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Ikeda Town, Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ikeda, Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[sn:Subasukiji Shrine]][[en:Subasukiji Shrine]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q65292740]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Suwaazuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suwaazuki shrine gate covered snow.jpg |location = [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]] [[Imadate District]] [[Ikeda Town, Fukui|Ikeda Town]] Inari 13-1 |ISO = JP-18 |LatitudeDeg = 35 |LatitudeMin = 53 |LatitudeSec = 10.4 |LongitudeDeg = 136 |LongitudeMin = 20 |LongitudeSec = 43.9 |Deities = Right Hall: {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}
Main Hall: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稻魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Left Hall: {{ill|Ōnotehime|ja|大野手比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnotehime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Associated Deities: {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta no Mikoto|ja|太田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} |SacredObject = |Rank = {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
[[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]
{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}
{{ill|Imperial offerings shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial offerings shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Buretsu |MainHall = {{ill|Miya-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Alias = |Festival = June 10 |LabelPosition = left |Map = Japan Fukui |location = {{ill|Ikeda|qid=Q1347548}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.88622222222222|N|136.3455277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Suwaazuki Shrine''' (須波阿湏疑神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Ikeda Town, Fukui|Ikeda Town]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]], Japan. The character "湏" (water radical + 頁) is not included in [[:en:JIS X 0208|JIS X 0208]], so the alternative representation "'''須波阿須疑神社'''" is sometimes used online, though it represents a different character. == Overview == Suwaazuki Shrine is identified as a candidate for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Suwaazuki Shrine Sanza (Echizen Province, Imadate District)" listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. It is classified as a small shrine in the Shikinaisha system and designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Cultural Properties == ; Main Hall : Built in the {{ill|Miya-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} style, it measures 4.88 meters in length (桁行) and 3.15 meters in width (梁間). It features a {{ill|Hiwadabuki|ja|檜皮葺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiwadabuki|qq=|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}} roof with a three-bay {{ill|Mokoshi|ja|向拝|1=kōhai|2=ja|3=向拝|4=zh|5=向拜|lt=kōhai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/kōhai (architecture)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415970}}. Constructed during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], it is the oldest shrine main hall in Fukui Prefecture. Designated as a {{ill|National Treasure of Japan (pre-1950)|ja|旧国宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Treasure of Japan (pre-1950)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国宝|1=National Treasure of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1139795}} under the 1941 National Treasure Preservation Law, it is now an Important Cultural Property under the 1950 Cultural Property Protection Law.Ministry of Education Notification No. 823, November 6, 1941 ; Worship Hall : Built during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] in 1689 (Genroku 2), it has a gabled roof, originally thatched and now copper-tiled. Measuring 8 by 5.5 bays, it has plain wooden paneling. The hall functioned as a [[:en:Noh|Noh]] stage until 1917 (Taishō 5) and is designated as a cultural property by the town. ; Gate (Rōmon) : A three-bay, one-entrance {{ill|Rōmon|ja|楼門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōmon|qq=|1=Rōmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378762}} with a gabled roof and copper tiles, originally thatched. Built in the late Edo period, it features white plastered walls and vermillion-painted wood, reflecting [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] practices. It is also designated a cultural property by the town. == Features == ; Shrine Treasures : A Noh mask of "Tenjin," dedicated in 1571 (Genki 2) by Chiyokumamaru from Nojiri, is recognized as an Important Art Object.Ministry of Education Notification No. 148, May 28, 1949 ; Noh Mask Festival : Although Noh performances are no longer held, an annual "Noh Mask Festival" takes place on February 6, featuring offerings of azuki porridge to visitors. ; Inari Giant Cedar : The sacred tree "Inari Giant Cedar" stands on the hillside behind the main hall. Dedicated to {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, it is named "Aratama Giant Cedar Daimyōjin." Estimated at 500 years old, it stands 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of 8.6 meters and a root spread of 11.7 meters. It is a Fukui Prefecture Natural Monument. == Religious Events == ; festival (Ikeda Grand Festival) : Held over three days around June 10, this festival has been celebrated since before the Edo period to pray for abundant harvests. * June 9: Performance of "Ikeda Oiwake," a Fukui Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * June 10 (main day): Morning prayer offering and tamagushi ritual; afternoon performances, including "Urayasu no Mai," sumo wrestling, and taiko drumming by groups from Echizen City. == History == The shrine was traditionally founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] with a division of {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}} from [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. Later, it enshrined the local deity "Azuki Kami" (Ōnotehime) and added Ukanomitama (Inari deity) in 716 (Reiki 2, Nara period). The main hall houses Ukanomitama, with Takeminakata and Ōnotehime in the right and left halls, respectively, and associated deities Ōta no Mikoto and Ōnamuchi. Historically, the shrine served as the communal shrine (Sōsha) of 48 villages in Ikeda and was revered by successive rulers. During the late Muromachi period, war caused a decline until 1491 (Entoku 3), when the shrine was restored by Ikeda Tokitada, a retainer of {{ill|Asakura Sadakage|ja|朝倉貞景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Sadakage|qq=|1=Asakura Sadakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517337}}. The current main hall dates from this restoration. However, in 1574 (Tenshō 2), the [[:en:Ikkō-ikki|Ikkō-ikki]] uprising destroyed most buildings and treasures, leaving only the main hall. Subsequent restoration began in 1595 (Bunroku 4), with the worship hall rebuilt by 1600 (Keichō 5). Silver donations from lords supported further repairs during the Edo period. The current gate was completed in 1850 (Kaei 3). The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1873 (Meiji 6) and became an Imperial offerings shrine in 1908 (Meiji 41). == location == Address: Fukui Prefecture, Imadate District, Ikeda Town, Inari 13-1 == Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Takefu Station|Takefu Station]], [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]]. ** Bus: Take the Ikeda Town route, disembark at "Inari," 2 minutes on foot. * By car: About 30 minutes from [[Takefu Interchange]] or [[Sabae Interchange]] on the [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]], or 35 minutes from [[Fukui Interchange]]. == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Noh Museum|ja|能楽の里歴史館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noh Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Displays related to Noh culture. * {{ill|Ukamaji Shrine|ja|鵜甘神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukamaji Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Known for its traditional dance. * {{ill|Horiguchi House|ja|堀口家住宅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horiguchi House|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Edo period structure. * {{ill|Kazura Bridge|ja|かずら橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazura Bridge|qq=|1=Kazura bridge at the Iya Valley|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11263836}} - Scenic vine suspension bridge. * {{ill|Ikeda Folklore Museum|ja|池田町民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Folklore Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Umada Clan Garden|ja|梅田氏庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umada Clan Garden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Historic garden of the Umada family. * {{ill|Noh Mask Art Museum|ja|能面美術館（福井県）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noh Mask Art Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.genbu.net/data/etizen/suhaasugi_title.htm Suwaazuki Shrine] - Genbushi no Kioku * [http://www.e-ikeda.jp/see/p004020.html Suwaazuki Shrine & Inari Giant Cedar] - Ikeda Town Tourism Information {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Fukui Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|須波阿湏疑神社|version=102326784|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Subasukiji Shrine]][[sn:Subasukiji Shrine]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] }} [[sn:Subasukiji Shrine]] [[ja:須波阿湏疑神社]] [[Category:Pages with merged translatations]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suwaazuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suwaazuki shrine gate covered snow.jpg|220px]]
Torii Gate | location = Inari 13-1, Ikeda Town, Imadate District, Fukui Prefecture, Japan | ISO = JP-18 | Latitude = 35.8862 | Longitude = 136.3455 | deity = Right Hall: {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}
Main Hall: {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}
Left Hall: {{ill|Ōnotehime-no-Kami|ja|大野手比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnotehime-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Accompanying Deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Okuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōta-no-Kami|ja|太田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|qq=}} | Classification = {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kensha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|Shrines receiving offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines receiving offerings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Buretsu | Architecture = Three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Festival = [[:en:June 10|June 10]] | Map = Japan Fukui | Label = left }} '''Suwaazuki Shrine''' (須波阿湏疑神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ikeda Town (Fukui)|ja|池田町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Town (Fukui)|qq=|1=Ikeda Town (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206815}}, Imadate District, Fukui Prefecture. The character "湏" (water radical + 頁) is not included in the [[:en:JIS X 0208|JIS X 0208]] character set, and the shrine is often referred to as "須波阿須疑神社" as a substitute notation online. == Overview == The shrine is considered the location of the "Suwaazuki Shrine Three Halls" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a small-scale {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. It was classified as a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kensha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Cultural Properties == === Main Hall === The Main Hall is a three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure, measuring 4.88 meters across the beams and 3.15 meters along the depth. It features a cypress bark roof and a three-bay porch. Built during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], it is the oldest shrine building in Fukui Prefecture and was designated as a National Treasure under the pre-war {{ill|National Treasure Preservation Law|ja|国宝保存法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Treasure Preservation Law|qq=|1=National Treasures Preservation Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420716}} in 1941. It has since been reclassified as an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Act|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Act|qq=|1=Act on Protection of Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188502}} in 1950.Showa 16 (1941), Ministry of Education Notification No. 823 === Worship Hall === The current Worship Hall was built in [[:en:1689|1689]] during the early Edo period. It features an irimoya (hip-and-gable) roof covered with copper sheeting (formerly thatched) and has a unique open layout used for Noh performances. It is designated as a cultural property by the town. === Gate (Rōmon) === The gate is a three-bay, single-entry structure with a hipped roof. The design reflects a combination of Shinto and Buddhist architectural styles. Built in the late Edo period, it is also recognized as a cultural property by the town. == Special Features == === Shrine Treasures === The shrine possesses a Noh mask called "Tenjin," which was donated in [[:en:1571|1571]]. This mask is classified as an Important Cultural Property.Showa 24 (1949), Ministry of Education Notification No. 148 === Noh Mask Festival === Although Noh performances are no longer held, the shrine organizes an annual "Noh Mask Festival" on [[:en:February 6|February 6]], where visitors are served azuki bean porridge. === Great Cedar of Inari === The sacred tree "Great Cedar of Inari" is located on the hillside behind the Main Hall. Estimated to be 500 years old, it is one of the most notable trees in the Hokuriku region and is designated a Natural Monument by Fukui Prefecture. == Festivals == === Grand Festival (Ikeda Grand Festival) === The Grand Festival is held annually around [[:en:June 10|June 10]] over three days. It includes traditional events like the offering of prayers for bountiful harvests. * June 9: Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property "Ikeda Oiwake." * June 10 (main day): Shinto rituals, including norito recitation and tamagushi offerings. * June 10–11: Various performances, including the "Dance of Peace" and taiko drum performances. == History == The shrine was traditionally founded during the reign of Emperor Buretsu by enshrining a divided spirit of {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} from the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. In the Nara period, local deity {{ill|Onotehime|ja|大野手比売|1=Ōnotehime-no-Kami|2=ja|3=大野手比売|lt=Ōnotehime-no-Kami|lt=Ōnotehime-no-Kami|ja|lt2=大野手比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onotehime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnotehime-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (an [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari deity]]) were also enshrined. Despite setbacks during the Sengoku period, including destruction by the [[:en:Ikkō-ikki|Ikkō-ikki]], the shrine was later reconstructed. In 1873, it was designated a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kensha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and became a shrine receiving state offerings in 1908. == location == '''Address:''' * Inari 13-1, Ikeda Town, Imadate District, Fukui Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation:''' * By train: Take the JR West Hokuriku Main Line to [[:en:Takefu Station|Takefu Station]]; from there, a bus to "Inari" and a two-minute walk. * By car: Accessible from the [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]] via Takefu or Sabae Interchanges (30 minutes). == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Nogaku no Sato Historical Museum|ja|能楽の里歴史館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogaku no Sato Historical Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Displays traditional Noh masks and costumes. * {{ill|Ugami Shrine|ja|鵜甘神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Features a unique tradition of Dengaku dance. * {{ill|Horiguchi Family Residence|ja|堀口家住宅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horiguchi Family Residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Edo-period architecture linked to the shrine. * {{ill|Kazura Bridge|ja|かずら橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazura Bridge|qq=|1=Kazura bridge at the Iya Valley|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11263836}} – A scenic suspension bridge. * {{ill|Ikeda Town Folklore Museum|ja|池田町民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Town Folklore Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Exhibits traditional agricultural tools and daily life artifacts. * {{ill|Umeda Family Garden|ja|梅田氏庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeda Family Garden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Historic garden of the Umeda family. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.genbu.net/data/etizen/suhaasugi_title.htm Suwaazuki Shrine – Genbushiko's Memories] * [http://www.e-ikeda.jp/see/p004020.html Suwaazuki Shrine & Inari Cedar – Ikeda Tourism Information] {{translated page|ja|須波阿湏疑神社|version=102326784|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] }} [[sn:Sunahaasu Shrine]] [[ja:須波阿湏疑神社]] [[sn:Subasukiji Shrine]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Ikeda Town, Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ikeda, Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[sn:Subasukiji Shrine]][[en:Subasukiji Shrine]]
